auth_notes: Requires a Personal Access Token generated from the Calendly Developer Portal. Note that n8n also supports OAuth2 for multi-user environments.
rate_limits: Standard rate limits are 30 requests per minute per user for most endpoints, with higher limits for Enterprise plans.
gotchas: API v1 is deprecated; ensure workflows use API v2 endpoints to avoid service interruption.
Timezone data is returned in UTC by default, which often requires a Date & Time node for conversion to the user's local timezone.
Webhook subscriptions are organization-wide or user-specific; misconfiguring the scope can lead to missing events or duplicate triggers.
research_notes: While 'Scheduling' is a category, the n8n native node is specifically built for Calendly. For Cal.com, a separate native node exists (n8n-nodes-base.calCom). Users should clarify if they need the open-source Cal.com or the enterprise Calendly solution.

effort_recommendation.rationale: While 'Scheduling' is a category, the n8n native node is specifically built for Calendly. For Cal.com, a separate native node exists (n8n-nodes-base.calCom). Users should clarify if they need the open-source Cal.com or the enterprise Calendly solution.
